title: fixed timestep for sim stepping in sim-layer
description: PR #2 review finding: SimLayer.update() steps the sim once per uncapped Pixi ticker frame (crit-engine.ts ticker), so on high-refresh displays the world sim runs faster than the dtMs-based crit-number overlay and they drift in real time. Acceptable for the demo bootstrap, but eruption timing and economy coupling in wave 3 need the sim on a fixed timestep (accumulator pattern, e.g. 20Hz to match the storm harness).

title: EV crossover assertions
description: three harness assertions: (1) EV crossover — riding EV-positive to n~6 undefended, n~10 defended; P(bust on next crit) crosses 1/3 near those points (pot x1.5 per crit vs total loss). (2) anti-farming — cores/min strictly increases with storm depth across the 8->35min arc; sqrt(essence) is concave, so flat essence growth makes tiny-storm spam optimal. (3) tune constants until all land. design.md §3, §6.

title: epic: economy tuning harness
description: headless storm simulator + EV assertions; no economy change ships without a harness run. design.md §6.

title: extend sim/run-sim.ts into headless storm simulator
description: bot strategies: never-ride, always-ride, bank-at-n; runs full storms without rendering.

title: brush purchase + paint UI (stone/water/ice/wall)
description: per-cell pricing; powder-lab brush painting adapted to purchase flow. stone/water are v0.1-slice materials (design.md §8) — ship those first; ice/wall follow later with the rest of the epic.

title: surge HUD: pot value, multiplier, core temp gauge
description: pot swell + multiplier + core temp gauge. no countdown timer anywhere; the gauge shows headroom in 'one more crit' units (does a median / max-tier spike still fit?). needed for the v0.1 slice — the bust is not a fair gamble if you can't read it. design.md §3, §8.

title: BANK action: pot mega-eruption
description: spacebar/button; entire pot erupts as gold mountain at current multiplier; surge ends, world cools.

title: overheat bust: pot erupts as lava + fire
description: core exceeds critical temp -> pot converts to lava/fire eruption; nearby world gold melts; the player watches it burn.

title: surge heating: per-crit tier spikes + ambient ramp, tier floor, critical temp
description: two heat sources: ambient +q*n^2 per second (mild, deterministic anti-stall clock) AND a per-crit core heat spike scaled by crit tier — tier is random, so the spike is random; the roll that pumps the pot is the one that can bust you. a deterministic ramp alone makes bank-at-95%-temp a dominant strategy and kills push-your-luck. surge crits start at tier>=1; core critical temp raised by aegis. design.md §3, §6.

title: epic: surge (bank-or-ride)
description: the core tension: pot + multiplier during surge, stochastic per-crit heat spikes + ambient ramp as the bust clock (physics is the theater, core temp gauge is the instrument), BANK spectacle vs overheat bust. design.md §3.

title: surge state machine: heat trigger, pot, multiplier 1.5^n
description: replaces frenzy. every strike contributes: non-crit strikes add base damage to the pot (no dead clicks inside the centerpiece), crits add payout and pump the multiplier; n = crits landed. the pot is never an abstract number (pillar 1): molten gold visibly swells inside the core as it grows. design.md §3.

title: collector: gold -> essence with fee
description: drain region converts arriving gold cells to essence at (1-fee), fee 30% base; HUD essence wiring replaces direct essence add in applyAttack.

title: per-cell value field with conservation
description: Float32Array parallel to grid, Lagrangian (property of the particle, unlike Eulerian heat). carry contract: swap() moves value with the cell; setCell() zeroes value unless the transition explicitly carries it (GOLD<->MOLTEN_GOLD preserve in place); destruction paths (acid, lava) zero and account as lost. eruption distributes P across cells; INGOT merge consumes neighbor cells + sums value; ingot melt-back spreads value across resulting molten cells (one cell must never carry a whole ingot). invariant test: sum(value field) + collected + lost === total erupted, every frame. design.md §4.1.

title: eruption spawner: pixi ballistic phase -> grid handoff
description: the sim has NO velocity (every cell moves <=1 cell/frame under gravity), so eruption flight is a pixi-layer projectile phase: arcs from core toward cursor with tier-scaled spread, converts to MOLTEN_GOLD grid cells on impact. m = clamp(4 + 6*log10(P), 4, 64); temp table by tier (design.md §6); tier 8 adds lightning + lava spray. design.md §7.

title: MOLTEN_GOLD + GOLD materials
description: add to material tables: density + movable entries (molten gold vs water/lava ordering matters — lava 90 must absorb it, it must sink through water 30), conductivity, melt 300 / freeze 150 transitions; lava absorbs molten gold (value lost); acid dissolves gold (value lost). MOLTEN_GOLD needs lava-style heat-carry on movement (heat is Eulerian; without re-seeding the destination cell a flowing stream reads cold and freezes mid-flight — see moveLava). design.md §4.1.
notes: also add INGOT: gold buried under >=N gold neighbors for a few seconds fuses into a static ingot cell worth the merged sum; melts back to molten gold. keeps big wealth visually dense (design.md §4.1).

title: epic: gold matter loop
description: payout as physical matter: molten gold eruptions, value conservation, collector banking. design.md §2, §4.1, §4.3.

title: render sim pixel buffer as pixi texture layer
description: nearest-neighbor upscaled texture as bottom layer of CritEngine stage; crit numbers/effects stay on top.

title: world bootstrap: grid, terrain floor, storm core, strike zone
description: ~320x180 grid; storm core entity at center; clicks in strike zone roll the crit chain.

title: epic: sim port & rendering
description: port powder-lab falling-sand sim into critstorm and render it under the pixi effects layer. design.md §7.

title: port Simulation.ts + materials.ts + tests into src/sim
description: copy from ~/git/powder-lab/src/sim. zero react deps BUT not headless as-is: Simulation constructor creates canvases (document.createElement) and renders via ImageData/canvas2D. split sim core (cells/heat/step, typed arrays, exposes raw pixel buffer) from a canvas render layer. the split is required by both the pixi texture path AND the headless harness (node has no document). keep vitest tests green against the extracted core. design.md §7.
close_reason: ported powder-lab Simulation/materials/scene into src/sim/; split headless sim core (simulation.ts) from canvas render adapter (simulation-renderer.ts); added vitest; 33 ported tests green, tsc clean, headless probe passes

title: wire world bootstrap into crit-engine; remove demo scene
description: Post-wave-2 integration gap. src/game/world.ts (createWorld) is merged but orphaned — no runtime consumer imports it (only world.test.ts). Meanwhile src/game/crit-engine.ts still constructs its own new Simulation(SIM_W,SIM_H) and calls paintDemoScene(this.sim), and src/game/sim-layer.ts still exports paintDemoScene. Both carry 'DEMO BOOTSTRAP — replaced by world bootstrap (b4r.3)' comments, but critstorm-b4r.3 is CLOSED, so no open issue tracks doing the swap or deleting the demo scene. Next wave should: replace the crit-engine sim/paintDemoScene wiring with createWorld().sim, drop the local SIM_W/SIM_H constants, remove paintDemoScene + its test, and consume world.core/strikeZone for the eruption spawner. Also fold the three duplicated 320x180 constant pairs (world DEFAULT_W/H, crit-engine SIM_W/H, storm-simulator DEFAULT_GRID_W/H) and the duplicated mulberry32 (sim/rng.ts vs a private divergent copy in world.ts using seed>>>0) into shared sources while touching this code.

title: anti-farming assertion in storm harness tests
description: PR #3 review finding: design.md §6 mandates economic assertions (EV crossover, anti-farming, bust-hazard) for the storm simulator. Crossover and bust-hazard are blocked on the hkm.* surge mechanics, but the anti-farming assertion depends only on existing essence growth and is implementable now against sim/storm-simulator.ts.

title: bust legibility: staged physical tell ladder
description: fixed, learnable escalation order as core temp climbs: ice sweats -> water steams -> plants smoke -> oil flashes -> world gold shimmers toward melt. the heat field's visual tint is +/-14 RGB — near invisible — so without deterministic staged tells the world goes from fine to on-fire with no warning and pillar 2's 'watch disaster approach' fails. design.md §3.

title: art direction pass: casino x pixel-native
description: slot-cabinet identity rebuilt in the pixel medium; kill any SaaS chrome; chrome recedes, sim is the star.

title: audio: eruption/quench/sizzle/bank synth layer
description: extend oscillator AudioEngine; map sim events (quench hiss, ignition, gold landing) to blips.

title: pacing tune: storm length + essence growth
description: essence/min ~x10 per 10 min; first storm ~8 min likely blow-up; late storms 25-35 min.

title: epic: presentation fusion
description: casino identity in pixel-native language; audio + juice for bank/bust moments. design.md §1 pillar 4.

title: workshop UI: Forge/Vault/Aegis/Front tracks
description: ~15 nodes per track, cost growth ~1.6; between-storm screen.

title: save/load: localStorage for cores + workshop
description: storms are ephemeral; only meta persists.

title: epic: meta layer
description: storm cores, workshop tracks, persistence. design.md §5.

title: storm cores conversion + bank-out bonus
description: cores = floor(sqrt(bankedEssence/500)) where bankedEssence is CUMULATIVE essence collected this storm (spending on in-storm upgrades does not reduce it — otherwise late-storm buying is a hoarding tax). x1.5 bank-out bonus applies to CORES after the sqrt (on essence it would only be x1.22 — too weak). minimum 1 core for any storm that reached its first surge. design.md §5.

title: storm fronts: flats, bog (glacier = post-v1 stretch)
description: terrain gen + hazard mix + payout modifiers per front. v1 ships flats + bog + the eye (npq.4); glacier is post-v1 stretch — the only front whose mechanic (collector flooding) needs new sim behavior instead of existing hazards. design.md §4.5.

title: storm end states: bank-out vs blow-up + results screen
description: voluntary bank-out (+50% core bonus) vs blow-up; stats screen either way.

title: epic: storm arc & finale
description: escalating events, end states, storm fronts, CRITSTORM finale. design.md §4.4-§4.6.

title: storm event system with escalating cadence
description: gold rain, acid drizzle, lava fissure, lightning front; severity scales with storm duration.

title: auto-striker structure
description: turret that strikes on a timer, replacing critstorm's attackRate upgrade. must arrive EARLY-MID storm, not endgame: with attackRate removed from in-storm upgrades, manual clicking is the only strike source until this exists — hands-on time should go to routing/defense, not meter-filling. design.md §2, §4.3.

title: magnet structure: gold routing
description: reuse sim magnet mechanic as placeable structure pulling gold powder toward it.

title: epic: defense brushes & structures
description: essence-priced painted materials and placed automation structures. design.md §4.2, §4.3.

title: gold loss rules + feedback
description: acid dissolve, lava absorb, melt-and-drain; each loss has visible/audible feedback so risk reads instantly.

title: perf pass: 60fps with sim + pixi together
description: budget check on desktop; sim step + texture upload + pixi effects. benchmark the worst case, not the flats: lava self-wakes every frame (updateLava calls wake unconditionally), so 'the eye' (lava floor) keeps most chunks permanently active and the dirty-chunk scheduler buys nothing. design.md §7.

title: clamp coreY in world.ts createWorld
description: PR #1 review finding: coreY = surface[coreX] - coreAboveFloor is not clamped to >= 0; a large coreAboveFloor or small floorLevel option produces a negative coreY, breaking the core-in-open-air/in-bounds invariant and causing an out-of-range sim.cells read. Defaults are safe and the bounds test only exercises defaults — clamp and add a non-default test.

title: validate numeric cli flags in sim/run-sim.ts
description: PR #3 review finding: --duration and --seed pass their argument straight to Number() with no validation; a missing or non-numeric value yields NaN, making totalFrames NaN so the frame loop runs zero times and prints an empty storm instead of erroring. --mode is validated; the numeric flags should error out on NaN the same way.

title: juice pass: bank + bust moments
description: the two clip-able moments; screen-scale feedback, slow-mo beat on bust, payout counter on bank.

title: the eye + CRITSTORM finale
description: lava floor, constant lightning; win = bank pot >= threshold at max multiplier; world-consuming golden storm + credits.

title: sprinkler + lightning rod structures
description: periodic water spray; lightning rod converts storm lightning to free max-tier strikes.
